markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement tags as first-class LoomBlocks/tag hubs with backlinks, nested tags, and search filters. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: TagsAndTagHubs leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TagsAndTagHubs is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Tags as first-class LoomBlocks (tag_hub) with sub-tags, backlinks, and tag search filtering over loom_blocks + loom_edges. No parallel store.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - impls + loom_blocks_by_incoming_edge helper; list_blocks_for_tag uses a cycle-guarded recursive CTE over SUB_TAG (child SOURCE -> parent TARGET) for nested-tag membership

tests: get_tag_hub_exposes_subtags_tagged_blocks_and_backlinks - #project hub shows #alpha sub-tag, direct-tagged N1, backlink_count=2 (SUB_TAG + TAG)
list_blocks_for_tag_resolves_nested_membership - direct => N1; include_subtags => N1 + N2 (via #alpha descendant)
tag_hub_apis_fail_closed_on_non_tag_block - get_tag_hub + list_blocks_for_tag reject a non-tag_hub block

evidence.summary: Crash recovery to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after independent adversarial review PASS and fresh current-tree real-PostgreSQL proof for Loom tag hubs/nested tags/backlinks/tag filters. This is Kernel Builder handoff readiness only; validator review remains pending.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

findings: SUB_TAG CYCLE (handled): the nested-tag recursive CTE carries a path array and excludes already-visited sources (NOT source = ANY(path)), so a cyclic sub-tag graph cannot loop.

NON-TAG-HUB (handled+tested): get_tag_hub and list_blocks_for_tag fail closed with a typed Validation error if the block is not a tag_hub. (create_loom_edge already enforces TAG/SUB_TAG targets are tag_hub.)
INCLUDE_SUBTAGS GUARD (handled+tested): the recursion's $4::BOOL = TRUE clause disables descendant expansion when include_subtags=false, so direct membership is exact.
INJECTION (clear): all binds parameterized; edge_type literals are fixed strings; no user string interpolated into SQL.
DOS (mitigated): list_tag_hubs / list_blocks_for_tag clamp limit to 1..500; the SUB_TAG closure is bounded by the workspace tag graph.
DETERMINISM (clear): explicit ORDER BY updated_at DESC, block_id ASC; DISTINCT prevents duplicate rows when a block is tagged via multiple descendant tags.
fixes: No code change required; findings are handled-by-design and tested.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-182 requires tags as first-class Loom tag hubs with backlinks, nested tags, and search filters delivered as Handshake-native projections over PostgreSQL (loom_blocks + loom_edges). The implementation delivers exactly that: a LoomTagHub type, three real Postgres-backed Database methods + backend navigation APIs (including a cycle-guarded recursive CTE for nested-tag membership), proven by 4 committed integration tests that run against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with no SQLite/mock fallback.
findings: commit c6bf160a is the sole MT-182 commit; +485 lines across loom.rs, mod.rs, postgres.rs, api/loom.rs, and a new test file (no /.GOV/ or out-of-scope paths; all within packet allowed_paths src/backend/handshake_core + tests).

storage/loom.rs:1029 LoomTagHub { block, sub_tags, tagged_blocks, backlink_count } models the tag-hub surface; matches Master Spec §7.1.1.7 / [LM-TAG-002] (tag = LoomBlock content_type=TAG_HUB, TAG edges) in v02.194 07-user-experience-and-development.md:238,244.

postgres.rs list_tag_hubs filters content_type='tag_hub'; get_tag_hub fails closed via StorageError::Validation('loom block is not a tag_hub') when block is not a TagHub, and aggregates direct SUB_TAG children, direct TAG sources, and a COUNT(*) backlink_count over loom_edges.

postgres.rs list_blocks_for_tag uses a RECURSIVE CTE over edge_type='sub_tag' (child source -> parent target) with a path-array cycle guard (NOT source_block_id = ANY(path)) and a $4::BOOL=TRUE gate that disables descendant expansion when include_subtags=false, giving exact direct membership; verified against the test assertions.
Enum-to-SQL string consistency verified: loom.rs maps TagHub->'tag_hub', Tag->'tag', SubTag->'sub_tag' (lines 64,365,366), matching the literal strings bound in postgres.rs queries.

PostgresDatabase is the ONLY concrete Database impl; the NotImplemented trait-default stubs added in mod.rs for these three methods are overridden and not reachable from the live API/test path (sub-rule 1 clean).

tests/loom_tag_hub_tests.rs (4 tests) runs against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support, which documents NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (SKIP on absent binaries, panic on any other failure) — real durable-authority proof (sub-rule 2 PASS).

Self-cert clean: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260614-175624, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION — implementer correctly handed off rather than signing COMPLETED (sub-rule 3 PASS).

Aside (non-blocking paperwork): the evidence-referenced log ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/pg-external-loom-wiki-20260614-205058/green-mt182-loom-tag-hub-tests.log is not present on disk (artifacts are external/transient); proof realism is established from the committed test code itself, not the convenience log.
evidence_checked: git show c6bf160a (full diff) in wtc-kernel-009
grep for deferred-live escapes across impl + test paths (none found)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
